name: Río Guadiana and Puente del Río Jabalón — circular from Corral de Calatrava
name_original: Río Guadiana i puente del Río Jabalón — circular desde el Corral de Calatrava
region_intro: Jogging around Corral De Calatrava offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by riverine paths along the Guadiana, historical trails, and greenways. The region features varied terrain, from flat sections suitable for easy jogging routes to more challenging trails with moderate elevation gains. Runners can explore a mix of natural and agricultural areas, providing a range of scenic backdrops for their runs. Best jogging routes around Corral De Calatrava The most popular jogging route is Río Guadiana and Puente del Río Jabalón — circular from Corral de Calatrava, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete, following riverbanks and passing historical bridges. Another top favourite among local runners is Maar of La Hoya del Mortero – Poblete Greenway loop from Poblete, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.5 km) path that traverses a greenway and features a unique volcanic maar. Local runners also love the Alcolea Trail loop from Alcolea de Calatrava, a 13.3 miles (21.4 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 53 minutes. Jogging around Corral De Calatrava is defined by river paths, greenways, and historical trails, with options available for moderate and advanced jogging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Corral De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Corral De Calatrava?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Corral De Calatrava listed on komoot, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels for joggers.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in the area?
A: While the guide lists mostly moderate to difficult routes, many sections of the riverine paths along the Guadiana offer relatively flat terrain suitable for easier runs. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210367513/rio-guadiana-and-puente-del-rio-jabalon-circular-from-corral-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Guadiana and Puente del Río Jabalón — circular from Corral de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.2 miles (13.2 km) and follows riverbanks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails near Corral De Calatrava?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, including riverine paths along the Guadiana, historical trails, and greenways. You'll find a mix of flat sections and more challenging trails with moderate elevation gains, traversing natural and agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17298928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar of La Hoya del Mortero – Poblete Greenway loop from Poblete&lt;/a&gt; is a 9.0-mile (14.5 km) circular path that includes a unique volcanic maar. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caracuel Castle – Corral de Calatrava loop from Corral de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;, which is a longer 11.3-mile (18.2 km) route.

Q: What are some interesting sights or landmarks I can see while running?
A: The routes often pass by historical and natural points of interest. You might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3882572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old dam of the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Hermitage and Maares Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3554273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails around Corral De Calatrava suitable for families?
A: Many of the greenways and flatter river paths can be enjoyable for families, especially those with older children. It's always best to check the specific route's distance and elevation profile on komoot to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most rural trails in Spain, especially if kept on a leash. However, always be mindful of local regulations, especially in protected natural areas or agricultural lands. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Corral De Calatrava?
A: Spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for running in Corral De Calatrava, with mild weather and beautiful natural scenery. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended if visiting during July and August. Winters are generally mild, making year-round running possible, though some days might be chilly.

Q: Are there any advanced or challenging running routes?
A: Yes, for more experienced runners, there are challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19411022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcolea Trail loop from Alcolea de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.3-mile (21.4 km) trail with significant elevation gain, often taking nearly 3 hours to complete. Another difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caracuel Castle – Corral de Calatrava loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Corral De Calatrava?
A: More than 80 runners have explored Corral De Calatrava's varied terrain using komoot. They often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverbanks to historical sites, and the mix of natural and agricultural areas that provide scenic backdrops for their runs.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of wildlife?
A: The riverine paths along the Guadiana and trails through natural areas are excellent for spotting local wildlife, particularly various bird species. Running early in the morning or late in the evening increases your chances of encountering animals in their natural habitat.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting from towns like Corral de Calatrava or Poblete, you can typically find street parking or designated parking areas within the village. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for more precise parking information.
tips: A place worth visiting, where the flow of the Guadiana can be well appreciated.
Nice landscape in Campo de Calatrava crossing the Jabalón River.

Corral de Calatrava is a municipality located in the heart of Campo de Calatrava and the Guadiana River and its tributary, the Jabalón River, speak its name. In addition, at the limit of its territory, the emblematic Castle of Caracuel stands out, which has a very remote historical background.

waypoints.name: Old dam of the Guadiana River
